scope.summary: Implement per-garment fit property controls (ease offsets per body region, tightness blend factor) stored on tailor_garment_fits rows, and expose real-time Stress, Strain, and Pressure heatmap visualization layers in the Tailor viewport for the fitted garment
acceptance_criteria: GarmentFitProperties type (ease_overrides: EaseOverrideMap, tightness_blend: f32 in [0,1]) is storable on tailor_garment_fits as a fit_props_json JSONB column
POST /api/tailor/garments/{gar_id}/fit-properties accepts GarmentFitProperties and updates the fit row for the current active avatar

The solver computes per-vertex stress (stretch ratio vs rest), strain (scalar from the stretch constraint lambda), and pressure (collision force magnitude) after each sim run and stores them as a Float32Array sidecar in tailor_garment_fits
The Tailor viewport exposes a heatmap_mode query param (stress | strain | pressure | off) that colorizes the garment mesh from the stored per-vertex sidecar
A model can read the heatmap summary (region max/mean values) via GET /api/tailor/garments/{gar_id}/fit-heatmap for self-correction
cargo test fit_heatmap::test_stress_computed_after_sim and fit_heatmap::test_strain_within_range both pass
scope.risk_if_missed: Operators and models have no feedback on whether a garment is too tight or too loose in specific body regions after fitting. This is MD's primary fit-quality feedback tool; without it, fit quality can only be assessed by visual inspection of the 3D render, eliminating actionable iteration on fit.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
